arcs.label: Bi-weekly check-in
arcs.tone_register: Casual, fluent, peer-tempered. The manager is checking in, not auditing. Short questions, plain words. Willing to go deeper if something opens, but no agenda performance.
anti_patterns: Agenda-heavy openers that read like a HR form.
Performative intimacy ('the real version', 'no filter', 'real talk') — sounds like an audition, not a check-in.
Pivoting to growth/career framing in a routine check-in.
Generic 'how do you feel about X' instead of locating the friction.

arc.label: Pulse
arc.intent: How is the last stretch sitting — fast read.

arc.label: Friction
arc.intent: Where is anything snagging — work, energy, people.

arc.label: Momentum
arc.intent: What's moving forward and what's stuck.

arc.label: Lift
arc.intent: Closer. What would make the next two weeks lighter or sharper.

arcs.label: Performance & feedback
arcs.tone_register: Direct, adult-to-adult. No softening-as-cushioning. The manager has a view; the employee has a view; the meeting reconciles them. Name things plainly without dressing them up.
anti_patterns: Softening the gap so much it disappears.
Leading the employee toward the manager's prewritten conclusion.
Closing on 'how do you feel about that' instead of a concrete commitment.

arc.label: Self-read
arc.intent: Their read of the last stretch before any manager view lands.

arc.label: Evidence
arc.intent: Anchor on observable moments, not impressions.

arc.label: Gap naming
arc.intent: Name the specific gap or pattern at issue.

arc.label: Cause
arc.intent: What's driving it from their side — capability, clarity, context, or capacity.

arc.label: Commit
arc.intent: Closer. A concrete behavioural change with a date.

arcs.label: Growth & career plan
arcs.tone_register: Aspirational, forward-leaning, generative. Future-tense. The employee is the protagonist of their own next chapter; the manager is a coach, not an HR checkbox.
anti_patterns: Round-robin axis coverage that never deepens a single thread.
Asking 'what skill do you want to develop' before knowing where they want to go.
Closing on 'what support do you need from me' without first earning the gap and the investment.
Reframing growth questions as role-clarity or feedback questions — that's a different meeting type.

arc.label: Anchor
arc.intent: Where they are now in role — what's solid, what's stretching.

arc.label: Aspiration
arc.intent: Where they want to go, concretely — named roles, named scopes, named outcomes.

arc.label: Gap
arc.intent: What's between here and there — skills, exposure, decisions, time.

arc.label: Investment
arc.intent: What has to change from them, from you as manager, from the org — and what gets traded off.

arc.label: Commitment
arc.intent: Closer. One concrete next move before the next conversation.

arcs.label: Something feels off
arcs.tone_register: Observation-first, opt-in, low-pressure. The manager names what they saw; the employee chooses whether and how to engage. No diagnosis, no leading, no probing for an emotion the employee hasn't named.
anti_patterns: Naming an emotion or diagnosis the employee hasn't named themselves.
Stacking 'what's wrong' probes without giving them an opt-out.
Treating the meeting like a performance review.

arc.label: Landing
arc.intent: Surface a no-pressure space to arrive.

arc.label: Observation
arc.intent: Name the observable shift, hand them the mic.

arc.label: Underneath
arc.intent: If they open the door, follow it — what's underneath.

arc.label: Support
arc.intent: Closer. What would help, if anything, right now.

arcs.label: Onboarding check-in
arcs.tone_register: Welcoming, orienting, low-bar. The new joiner is still forming a map of the place — make it safe to admit confusion. Short, concrete, helpful. The manager is a guide removing friction, not assessing performance.
anti_patterns: Evaluating performance in the first weeks instead of removing friction.
Assuming silence means things are fine — new joiners under-report blockers.
Overloading them with information instead of surfacing what they actually need next.

arc.label: Settling
arc.intent: How they're landing — environment, tools, people, the basics.

arc.label: Orientation
arc.intent: What's still unclear — role, expectations, who's who, how things work here.

arc.label: Blockers
arc.intent: What's slowing them down or missing to actually do the work.

arc.label: Connection
arc.intent: Closer. Who they've connected with, and what support to line up next.
